title: Xanthic Dense Filipino Chickpeas Dinuguan
description: A sour and dense Filipino dish made with chickpeas and long-fermentation-dough, perfect for meal prep. This satvik-friendly dish is a unique twist on traditional dinuguan.

ingredients.name: long-fermentation-dough
ingredients.notes: made with rice flour and water

instructions: Soak the chickpeas in water overnight and cook them until they are tender.
Make the long-fermentation-dough by mixing rice flour and water, and letting it ferment for at least 24 hours.
Chop the onion and mince the garlic, and saute them in a pan until they are softened.
Add the vinegar, salt, and black pepper to the pan, and stir to combine.
Add the cooked chickpeas and long-fermentation-dough to the pan, and stir to combine.
Add the bay leaves to the pan, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for at least 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the dinuguan hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
